confidence.disclosure: The celebrated 1477-78 Syria-Cilicia inspection tour is documented day-by-day in Ibn al-Ji'an's al-Qawl al-Mustazraf fi Safar Mawlana al-Malik al-Ashraf, written by a chancery official who accompanied the sultan. The sixteen major military campaigns and building projects of the reign are summarised in Ibn Iyas's Bada'i' al-Zuhur and al-Sakhawi's al-Daw' al-Lami. Campaign movements during the Ottoman wars (1485-91) are less precisely dated than the 1477-78 tour.

sources: Ibn al-Ji'an, al-Qawl al-Mustazraf fi Safar Mawlana al-Malik al-Ashraf (1478)
Ibn Iyas, Bada'i' al-Zuhur fi Waqa'i' al-Duhur (c. 1522)
Al-Sakhawi, al-Daw' al-Lami li-Ahl al-Qarn al-Tasi (c. 1498)
Carl Petry, Twilight of Majesty: The Reigns of the Mamluk Sultans al-Ashraf Qaytbay and Qansuh al-Ghawri (1993)

journey.event: Born a Circassian in the Caucasus around 1416.
Purchased as a young mamluk by Sultan Barsbay in Cairo.
Enthroned as sultan on 31 January 1468 following the death of Yalbay.
Visited Alexandria to inspect the fortifications and shipyards and ordered the construction of the Fort of Qaitbay on the site of the Pharos.
Departed Cairo on 2 October 1477 at the head of a large escort for a provincial inspection tour of Syria and Cilicia.
Stopped at Gaza, the first Syrian provincial capital on the coastal route.
Passed through Ramla, the administrative centre of Palestine district.
Visited Jerusalem and prayed at al-Aqsa and the Dome of the Rock; ordered repairs to the Haram al-Sharif.
Continued north through Nablus.
Arrived in Damascus in late October 1477 and stayed several weeks inspecting the citadel and administration.
Detoured to Baalbek to inspect the citadel.
Visited Tripoli on the Lebanese coast.
Passed through Hama on the way north.
Arrived at Aleppo, the capital of the northern Mamluk provinces, and inspected its citadel.
Continued to the Anatolian frontier at Ayntab (modern Gaziantep).
Returned to Aleppo in January 1478 to begin the return journey.
Returned to Cairo in February 1478 after four months on the road.
War broke out with the Ottomans over the Dulkadir principality; Qaitbay sent armies north to the Anatolian frontier.
Mamluk forces defeated the Ottomans at the Battle of Ağaçayırı near Adana.
Signed peace with the Ottomans after six years of inconclusive border warfare.
Died in Cairo on 7 August 1496 after a 28-year reign, the longest of any Circassian Mamluk sultan.
